figures.caption: Figure 4 Study 2 (LLM adoption): forecast versus ground truth in a representative episode. The solid line shows realized adoption increments ∆t (likes/day). CDLF is shown under two cold-start settings: pre-launch (t0 = 1) and early post-launch (t0 = 6), with medians distinguished by marker and line style and P10– P90 predictive bands. The vertical dotted line marks t0.
Figure 7 Actionable playbook from generative launch segmentation. Each quadrant maps potential and risk segments to release actions, with candidate repositories listed in each quadrant.

Figure D.1 Numerical validation of the stability-based error propagation theory (estimated with R= 104 rollouts). (a) Evolution of the latent-state error Êt under stable vs. unstable configurations, including the damping of an injected pulse at t= 10, consistent with Lemma EC.1. (b) Evolution of the expected one-step discrepancy ∆̂t, showing bounded behavior in the stable regime (κ < 1) and divergence in the unstable regime (κ> 1), consistent with Theorem 1.
Figure 1 The pipeline of product sales life-cycle forecasting and the associated information phases.
Figure 3 Study 1: quantile-wise calibration measured by mean pinball loss across 99 quantiles.

Figure 6 Study 2 (LLM adoption): (a) typical adoption load versus breakout potential, with point size indicating uncertainty (P90–P10 AUC); (b) potential–risk segmentation for LLM repositories based on median splits of P50 AUC and AUC bandwidth.
Figure 2 Comparison Schematic of Diffusion Modeling Paradigms for Images and Time Series Forecasting

Table 1 Baseline families and cold-start design requirements. R1: no within-product history (Pre-Launch); R2: conditions on analog life-cycles and static product descriptors; R3: generates coherent life-cycle samples (supporting peak timing, peak magnitude, and cumulative adoption); R4: uses a single model that updates as a prefix arrives.
Figure 5 Study 2 (LLM adoption): pre-launch adoption curves conditioned on s = (Org,ScaleBucket,Gated). Curves show medians with P10–P90 bands, contrasting high vs. low exposure and friction.
Table 5 Study 2 (LLM adoption): ablation on conditioning sources. All metrics are computed on the forecast horizon [t0, T ] with T = 10 and t0 = 6 and averaged over rolling windows.
Table 3 Study 2 (Hugging Face LLM adoption): selected LLM repositories in the public trending panel.
